as_built_projection_spec_shape.public_rust: ProjectionSpec { name: String, roles: BTreeSet<ProjectionRole>, fts: Option<ProjectionFts>, vector: Option<ProjectionVector> }
as_built_projection_spec_shape.projection_role: enum ProjectionRole { Filterable, Rankable, Searchable } — EXACTLY three members; searchable->FTS / searchable->vector are tier labels carried by the fts/vector sub-objects, NOT enum members
as_built_projection_spec_shape.roles_semantics: Set semantics (BTreeSet: dedup + membership + order-independent). An attribute can be Filterable AND Searchable.
as_built_projection_spec_shape.fts_sub_object: ProjectionFts { tokenizer: Option<String> } — presence selects the searchable->FTS sub-target; None tokenizer = engine default
as_built_projection_spec_shape.vector_sub_object: ProjectionVector { embedder: Option<String> } — STORED by 15d (registry columns vector_declared + vector_embedder), builds nothing here
as_built_projection_spec_shape.delta: ProjectionDelta { built, dropped, deferred: Vec<String>, unchanged: bool }; destructive change is Err(ProjectionDestructive{name,delta}), not a delta
as_built_projection_spec_shape.dense_readiness_attach_point: Slice 20 (R-20-DR) adds a `dense_readiness` field to ProjectionVector (the `vector` sub-object). The on-disk registry already round-trips the sub-object via _fathomdb_projection_registry.vector_declared + .vector_embedder, so the attach is purely additive — no persisted-shape change is needed in Slice 20. StoredProjection::to_spec/from_spec and the SDK ProjectionSpec.vector flag are the seam.

eav_property_fts_schema_step_24.no_data_migration: HITL 2026-07-21 — shape only; tables come up EMPTY; configure_projections + boot re-derive populate them
tables: _fathomdb_projection_registry(name PK, roles TEXT, fts_tokenizer TEXT nullable [NULL=no fts / ''=fts+default tokenizer / non-empty=custom], vector_embedder TEXT nullable, vector_declared INTEGER) — durable derived-cache source (Q5)

canonical_attributes(write_cursor INTEGER, attr_name TEXT, attr_value TEXT) + index (attr_name, attr_value) + index (write_cursor) — row-owned EAV attribute store, values derived from node body JSON $.<name>, rebuild-durable
property_search_index USING fts5(attr_value, attr_name UNINDEXED, write_cursor UNINDEXED, tokenize='porter unicode61 remove_diacritics 2') — row-owned property-FTS shadow

row_owned_projections_added.also: both classes added to truncate_all_row_projections so a full rebuild clears+re-derives them; erase_row_projections (purge/excise_source) now reaches attribute values at rest
row_owned_projections_added.guard_proof: lib test guard_row_owned_registry passes with both tables registered; net-new lib test slice15d_attribute_projections_registered_and_guard_bites proves (1) both are registered and (2) the guard predicate REJECTS either if hypothetically unregistered. Engine test erase_source_reaches_attribute_projections asserts erased attribute values do not survive on disk in canonical_attributes or property_search_index.

reregistration_noop_and_explicit_drop.idempotent_noop: idempotent_reregistration_is_a_noop: second apply of the identical spec => ProjectionDelta.unchanged=true, built/dropped/deferred empty (StoredProjection PartialEq diff)
reregistration_noop_and_explicit_drop.explicit_drop: role_add_builds_and_explicit_drop_drops_exactly_one: omission of a live projection does NOT drop (C3); an explicit drop:[name] removes exactly that name's registry row + EAV rows + property-FTS rows; the un-dropped projection is untouched
reregistration_noop_and_explicit_drop.destructive_refusal: destructive_change_requires_explicit_drop: removing a role (or changing tokenizer/embedder) without a drop => Err(ProjectionDestructive); with drop:[name] it rebuilds fresh. A refused change does not partially apply.
reregistration_noop_and_explicit_drop.boot_rederive: boot_rederive_converges_after_simulated_crash: registry row survives but EAV/property-FTS rows wiped => next open rebuilds them from canonical state; a second open does not duplicate (idempotent). rederive_projections_on_boot runs on the writer connection after ensure_vector_partition, gated on registry-table existence (safe on every pre-24 schema).

typescript.result: 18 passed by execution in a fresh clone

x1_parity.allowlist: +configure_projections, +configureProjections, +read.projections — recorded PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED (AC-079 UNMINTED; gated by requirement-id + TDD test names). recovery_denylist unchanged (five names).

contract_or_plan_deviations: The EAV store is DERIVED FROM the node body JSON ($.<name>), not populated by a net-new attribute-write verb. Rationale: no attribute-write verb is in 15d scope (the Memex set_entity_attribute repoint is >=0.9.x / out of scope per C-1 Q2), yet R-20-EAV requires a populated, testable EAV store. Deriving from the body makes the store rebuild-durable (satisfies projection-registry §2 'projections are re-derivable from the canonical graph') AND row-owned/erasable, and reuses the shipped 'extract $.status from body' pattern (search_index_v2). The registry drives which attributes are extracted. This threads the C-1 contract ('the canonical interpretive values live in engine storage') without inventing an out-of-scope write verb.

`canonical_attributes` is named with the `canonical_` prefix but is classified as a ROW-OWNED PROJECTION (in ROW_OWNED_PROJECTIONS, NOT in schema CANONICAL_TABLES). This is deliberate: it holds attribute VALUES at rest that must be erased with the node, so it must be reachable by erasure. The name reflects that it holds the canonical attribute values; the classification reflects that they are rebuild-durable from the body.

fts_tokenizer registry column is tri-valued (NULL=no fts sub-object, ''=fts+engine-default, non-empty=custom) to durably distinguish `searchable+FTS with default tokenizer` from `searchable with no FTS`. Custom tokenizers are recorded but NOT honoured in 15d (the property-FTS table uses the default tokenizer); per-attr custom tokenizers are the >=0.9.x multi-field FTS work, grafting later via the same idempotent configure_projections (same graceful-graft as rankable / Q6a).

read.projections is exposed as a read-namespace verb in BOTH bindings (single allowlist name `read.projections`), while configure_projections is a top-level Engine method (two allowlist names for the two casings). This matches api-surface.md (configure_projections is a verb; read.projections is folded into read.*).

fix-1.title: codex §9 [P2]x2 — non-string scalar projection dropped + superseded projection rows never purged

red_evidence.result: test result: FAILED. 0 passed; 2 failed; 0 ignored; 11 filtered out
red_evidence.finding_1_panic: scalar_json_attributes_project_number_bool_and_string @ tests/slice15d_projection_registry.rs:558 — assertion `left == right` failed: a JSON number attribute must project into canonical_attributes; left: [] right: ["3"]  (the JSON number 3 was silently dropped)
red_evidence.finding_2_panic: supersession_purges_stale_attribute_projection_rows @ tests/slice15d_projection_registry.rs:634 — assertion `left == right` failed: only the active value must survive at rest; left: ["closed", "open"] right: ["closed"]  (the superseded 'open' row survived at rest)

finding_1_fix.cause: extraction read as Option<String>; for a JSON number/bool json_extract returns INTEGER/REAL, row.get::<Option<String>> FAILED the type conversion, and .unwrap_or(None) silently treated the attribute as absent — no canonical_attributes / property_search_index row.
finding_1_fix.fix: replaced the SELECT with a json_type-keyed CASE that renders exactly one canonical TEXT form per JSON type; the single `value` binding then flows to BOTH canonical_attributes and property_search_index, so the two tables are consistent by construction.

scalar_bool_null_representation.integer: decimal text via CAST(json_extract AS TEXT) — e.g. 3 -> "3"
scalar_bool_null_representation.real: decimal text via CAST(json_extract AS TEXT) — e.g. 3.5 -> "3.5"
scalar_bool_null_representation.bool: true -> "true", false -> "false" — the JSON literal, NOT SQLite's 1/0. Rationale: a bool filter should match the value the caller wrote, and it avoids a bare CAST collapsing JSON true and the number 1 to the same "1".
scalar_bool_null_representation.null: JSON null OR absent path -> SQL NULL -> NO row (an absent attribute correctly never matches a filterable equality — same semantics as before)
scalar_bool_null_representation.object_array: DELIBERATELY skipped -> SQL NULL -> no row. A composite value is not a scalar filter/FTS target in 15d; nested-field filtering is the >=0.9.x multi-field work. Skipping is deliberate (explicit CASE arm), not an accidental type-conversion drop — no scalar type is dropped.

finding_2_fix.site: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs — commit_batch node write arm (the logical_id supersession UPDATE) + two new helpers
finding_2_fix.cause: the write path marked the prior canonical_nodes row superseded_at but never removed its canonical_attributes / property_search_index rows; a same-session property filter / property-FTS saw BOTH the stale and current value until a boot re-derive / reconfigure cleared the table (violates the active-only projection invariant).
finding_2_fix.approach: PURGE-ON-SUPERSEDE (the mandate's preferred option). Before the tombstone UPDATE the write path collects the prior active cursor(s) via new prior_node_cursors_by_logical_id (mirrors prior_edge_cursors_by_logical_id; partial-unique-active => at most one), then after the UPDATE purges each via new purge_row_projections_for_cursor_in(tx, cursor, [Attribute, PropertyFts]).
finding_2_fix.why_purge_not_read_side: property_search_index is an FTS5 table with NO way to carry a `canonical_nodes WHERE superseded_at IS NULL` join, and property filters may read canonical_attributes directly (like vector_default) — so a read-side active-only filter cannot cover the FTS path. Purge-on-supersede keeps the at-rest table truthful.
finding_2_fix.class_scoping: purge is scoped to Attribute + PropertyFts ONLY. NodeFts (search_index / search_index_v2) + Vector shadows are LEFT INTACT because the node read path already excludes their superseded rows via the canonical_nodes superseded_at IS NULL join; purging them here would be a behaviour change outside this fix's scope.
finding_2_fix.consistency_with_erasure: purge_row_projections_for_cursor_in is the registry-driven sibling of erase_row_projections — it iterates ROW_OWNED_PROJECTIONS filtered by class and deletes by the declared cursor_column, so an unregistered content table is unreachable here exactly as with erasure. guard_row_owned_registry unchanged; erasure tests (erasure_projection_registry 10, erasure_completeness 4, excise_source 6, rebuild_projections 3) all green.
finding_2_fix.at_rest_active_only_proof: supersession_purges_stale_attribute_projection_rows asserts on RAW tables: after rewriting L=open->L=closed, eav_values('status')==["closed"], and COUNT(*) WHERE write_cursor=<superseded cursor> is 0 in BOTH canonical_attributes and property_search_index; eav_filter('status','open')==[] and property_fts_match('status','open')==[]; closed maps only to the active cursor. The superseded/active cursors are read from canonical_nodes (not hardcoded).

fix-1.governed_surface: UNCHANGED — no new verb, no AC minted, PROPOSED/NOT-SIGNED status of Slice 15d's own delta untouched. recovery_denylist stays five.

fix-2.title: codex §9 [P2] — the same-transaction attribute projection ignored lifecycle visibility for pending nodes (exposed rows the canonical read model hides)
fix-2.finding: The write-path call to project_node_attributes (lib.rs, PreparedWrite::Node arm) projected into canonical_attributes / property_search_index UNCONDITIONALLY, even for a node written InitialState::Pending. The backfill (backfill_attribute) and boot re-derive only include `state='active' AND superseded_at IS NULL` rows. So a pending (quarantined) node's declared attribute values leaked into same-session property filters / property-FTS — surviving until a boot re-derive or reconfigure cleared them. Also: the `transition` verb never maintained the property store, and the operator rebuild (rebuild_shadow_state) re-projected EVERY canonical row (re-surfacing pending + superseded attribute values).
fix-2.root_framing: The at-rest attribute projection must track EXACTLY the backfill's row set: projected ⟺ (state='active' ∧ superseded_at IS NULL). fix-1 enforced the supersession half at write time (purge-on-supersede); fix-2 enforces the existence/state half and keeps the invariant across the FULL lifecycle (write path + every legal transition + operator rebuild). Maintained as at-rest work (consistent with fix-1 + the backfill), NOT a read-side join — the property_search_index FTS5 table cannot carry a `superseded_at IS NULL` / `state='active'` join, and property filters may read canonical_attributes directly, so a read-side filter cannot cover the property paths.

commits.summary: test(0.8.20): RED — Slice 15d fix-2 lifecycle-gated projection (3 new engine tests in slice15d_projection_registry.rs)
feat(0.8.20): GREEN — projection tracks active+non-superseded across the write path, transition verb, and operator rebuild (+1 operator rebuild-consistency test)

red_evidence.result: test result: FAILED. 0 passed; 3 failed; 0 ignored; 13 filtered out
red_evidence.panic_1_write_gate: pending_node_is_not_projected_until_promoted @ slice15d_projection_registry.rs:828 — panicked: a pending node must NOT project attributes into canonical_attributes (the write path projected the Pending node's attribute unconditionally).
red_evidence.panic_2_delete_purge: active_delete_purges_and_undelete_reprojects @ slice15d_projection_registry.rs:906 — panicked: soft-delete (active → deleted) must purge canonical_attributes rows (the transition verb left stale attribute rows at rest).
red_evidence.panic_3_reject_gate: pending_reject_projects_nothing @ slice15d_projection_registry.rs:957 — panicked: pending write projects nothing (same write-path gate gap — pending values present at rest).

default_feature: pending_node_is_not_projected_until_promoted — a Pending node's declared attribute is NOT in canonical_attributes / property_search_index at write (raw SELECT == 0); then transition(pending→active) projects it (raw SELECT now present + filter/FTS matchable). Mid-session raw reads (WAL readers see committed data).

active_delete_purges_and_undelete_reprojects — CONTROL: an active node projects at write (non-vacuous); transition(active→deleted) purges the attribute/property-FTS rows (raw == 0); transition(deleted→active) re-projects (raw present again).
pending_reject_projects_nothing — a pending node projects nothing; transition(pending→deleted) leaves the projection empty (no spurious row, no error) — the never-projected arm of the unified rule.
operator_feature: slice15d_fix2_rebuild_projects_only_active_non_superseded_attributes (tests/rebuild_projections.rs) — after a full rebuild_projections(), the raw canonical_attributes hold EXACTLY {active-val, new-val}; a pending node's value and a superseded prior version's value are NOT re-surfaced; property_search_index mirrors the set. Proves the projector-replay rebuild tracks the backfill's row set.

fix_sites.write_path_gate: project_canonical_node_row gains a `node_active: bool` param; the attribute projection is now `if pass.writes_attributes() && node_active`. The FTS/vector shadows are UNCHANGED (their stale/pending versions are excluded by the canonical read-path lifecycle join — same class-scoping as fix-1). The PreparedWrite::Node write arm passes `matches!(state, InitialState::Active)` (a fresh insert is always non-superseded, so the backfill predicate reduces to state==Active).
fix_sites.rebuild_consistency: CanonicalNodeRow gains `attr_projected` = (state.as_deref()==Some("active") && superseded_at.is_none()) — the exact backfill predicate; canonical_node_rows now SELECTs state + superseded_at. rebuild_shadow_state passes row.attr_projected so the attribute half of the projector replay tracks active∧non-superseded (FTS/vector still replay every row). The always-compiled tokenizer-reproject caller forwards row.attr_projected too (inert under FtsOnly, but gives the field a reader in every build config → no dead_code warning). The doc-hidden coverage/graph test writer passes `true` (its INSERT uses the column DEFAULT state='active').
fix_sites.transition_maintenance: transition() now SELECTs (state, write_cursor, body) of the single non-superseded row. After the state UPDATE (before commit) it PURGES the row's Attribute+PropertyFts projections unconditionally (idempotent — a no-op on the never-projected pending / already-purged deleted arms) via fix-1's purge_row_projections_for_cursor_in, then RE-PROJECTS via project_node_attributes when to_state==Active. Unified rule (projected ⟺ to_state==Active, valid because the transitioned row is always non-superseded): promote (pending→active) projects the withheld attrs; soft-delete (active→deleted) purges; undelete (deleted→active) re-projects; reject (pending→deleted) is a no-op.

transitions_hooked.pending_to_active_promote: PROJECT — project_node_attributes(tx, cursor, body) after the UPDATE (attributes were withheld while pending).
transitions_hooked.active_to_deleted_soft_delete: PURGE — purge_row_projections_for_cursor_in(tx, cursor, [Attribute, PropertyFts]) (a deleted node is out of the backfill set).
transitions_hooked.deleted_to_active_undelete: RE-PROJECT — purge (no-op; already purged on delete) then project.
transitions_hooked.pending_to_deleted_reject: NO-OP — purge is a harmless no-op (never projected); no project. Confirmed by pending_reject_projects_nothing.

fix-2.invariant_confirmation: The at-rest attribute projection now tracks EXACTLY (state='active' AND superseded_at IS NULL) at every maintenance point: the write path (state==Active), each legal transition (unified purge-then-reproject-if-active), and the operator rebuild (per-row attr_projected mirrors the backfill predicate). fix-1 already covers supersession at write time. Node-FTS / vector shadows are deliberately left to their read-side lifecycle filter (gap-5: a deleted row STAYS indexed), unchanged.
fix-2.validity_scope_guard: CONFIRMED — NO node-validity (valid_from / valid_until, Slice 10) filtering was added to the projection. The backfill does not filter on validity, so the 15d projection model stays existence-based (active/superseded), not validity-based. attr_projected keys ONLY on state + superseded_at. Consideration (not built): if validity-window visibility should ever gate the property store, it must be added to the backfill FIRST (else a new write-path-vs-backfill inconsistency) — out of scope for fix-2.

commits.summary: test(0.8.20): RED — Slice 15d fix-4 projection-name validation (1 new engine test backslash_projection_name_is_rejected)

finding_1.site: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/src/lib.rs — is_valid_attribute_name (called by apply_projection_config for every spec.name AND every drop name) + attribute_json_path
finding_1.cause: is_valid_attribute_name guarded only empty / double-quote / NUL. A name carrying a BACKSLASH (e.g. `a\b`) was ACCEPTED, but the write-path extraction compiles the SQLite JSON path `$."<name>"` (double-quoted key) and SQLite treats `\` as an escape introducer inside that quoted key, so a body key literally containing `\` is NOT matched — the attribute was registered (built) yet silently NEVER populated canonical_attributes / property_search_index. Accept-then-never-populate.
finding_1.choice: REJECT AT THE BOUNDARY (the mandate's preferred option). Chosen over escaping because SQLite JSON-path double-quoted-key escape handling is version-sensitive and hard to prove round-trips across versions; a charset restriction on app-declared projection identifiers is a legitimate contract and mirrors the TC-33 hard-reject philosophy (accept ⟹ works). is_valid_attribute_name now rejects: empty, any double-quote, any BACKSLASH, and any ASCII control char (is_control, which subsumes the prior NUL check). Both the spec-name loop and the drop-name loop in apply_projection_config already call it, so both arms are covered; the typed refusal is the EXISTING EngineError::InvalidArgument naming the offending value — no new error variant.
finding_1.no_new_error_variant: InvalidArgument already exists (lib.rs:3275) and already maps to InvalidArgumentError in both bindings (lib.rs:3431). Only the validation predicate tightened.

finding_2.cause: fathomdb/src/lib.rs:46-47 re-exports the five Projection* types (ProjectionDelta, ProjectionFts, ProjectionRole, ProjectionSpec, ProjectionVector) making them public Rust facade; python.md/typescript.md carry a '## Projection registry' section but rust.md had none — violating the AGENTS.md:25 / TC-39 same-change invariant for a public Rust API surface.
finding_2.fix: Added a '## Projection registry (0.8.20 Slice 15d, R-20-PR / C-1)' section (PROPOSED / NOT SIGNED) documenting Engine::configure_projections + Engine::read_projections signatures and all five Projection* types (matching python.md/typescript.md detail, Rust-spelled), PLUS the fix-4 projection-name contract, and a one-line PROPOSED note in the P1 allowlist narrative (mirrors the ReadView/BoundaryCrossing precedent).
finding_2.reexport_list_verified: CONFIRMED against fathomdb/src/lib.rs:42-50 always-present re-export block: the five Projection* names documented are EXACTLY those re-exported (ProjectionDelta, ProjectionFts, ProjectionRole, ProjectionSpec, ProjectionVector); none is invented and none re-exported is missed. Type field/variant shapes cross-checked against the pub defs at lib.rs:3554-3643.

red_evidence.panic: backslash_projection_name_is_rejected @ tests/slice15d_projection_registry.rs:990 — called `Result::unwrap_err()` on an `Ok` value: ProjectionDelta { built: ["a\\b"], dropped: [], deferred: [], unchanged: false }
red_evidence.interpretation: RED proves the accept-then-never-populate path: the backslash name `a\b` was ACCEPTED (returned Ok with built:["a\b"], i.e. registered) instead of refused — a registry entry that the write-path JSON path can never populate.

fix-5.title: codex §9 [P2]x2 (one class) — the new configure_projections bindings skipped the AC-068 FFI-string gate on ProjectionSpec string fields
fix-5.finding: AC-068a/b requires every string crossing the FFI to be rejected (via validate_ffi_string) BEFORE the writer transaction opens. Both configure_projections binding paths copied the ProjectionSpec string fields into the Rust spec WITHOUT the gate: napi src/rust/crates/fathomdb-napi/src/lib.rs ProjectionSpec::to_rust (:504-508) and pyo3 src/rust/crates/fathomdb-py/src/lib.rs PyProjectionSpec::to_rust (:889-895). A NUL in fts_tokenizer / vector_embedder was persisted in _fathomdb_projection_registry (the two named P2s); a NUL in name / drop surfaced only the engine-layer InvalidArgument (fix-4), not the AC-068 binding-layer WriteValidationError required before the writer txn opens.

commits.summary: test(0.8.20): RED — Slice 15d fix-5 FFI validation on projection spec (4 Python + 4 TS binding tests)

the_fix.napi: ProjectionSpec::to_rust now runs validate_ffi_string_napi on self.name, each role string (before from_str_opt), Option fts_tokenizer, and Option vector_embedder; configure_projections validates each drop entry — all BEFORE call_engine (before the writer transaction).
the_fix.pyo3: PyProjectionSpec::to_rust now runs validate_ffi_string_py on self.name, each role string, Option fts_tokenizer, and Option vector_embedder; the configure_projections pyfunction validates each drop entry — all BEFORE call_engine.
the_fix.defense_in_depth: Engine-side is_valid_attribute_name (fix-4) still rejects unsafe projection NAMES; fix-5 is the BINDING-layer gate that refuses the FULL spec string set BEFORE the writer transaction opens (AC-068) — both layers intact, no engine-side change.
the_fix.no_new_error_variant: Reuses the existing WRITE_VALIDATION typed error (WriteValidationError / FDB_WRITE_VALIDATION) already mapped in both bindings — no new variant, no governed-surface NAME change.

boundary_contract_audit.scope: Proactively audited the NEW projection surface (configure_projections + read.projections, both bindings) against the repo-wide binding contracts. No expansion into unrelated verbs.
boundary_contract_audit.ffi_string_validation: CLOSED by fix-5 for NUL on BOTH bindings, typed as WriteValidationError before the writer txn. read.projections takes NO caller strings (only the engine handle) → nothing to validate; it returns engine-owned specs.
boundary_contract_audit.ac_068b_surrogate_residual: Lone UTF-16 surrogate (AC-068b) is REJECTED and NEVER persisted on BOTH bindings (verified: _fathomdb_projection_registry COUNT(*)=0). napi types it as WriteValidationError via the same validate_ffi_string_napi 0xD800..=0xDFFF range check used on body/kind/filter. pyo3 rejects it EARLIER — at the PyProjectionSpec pyclass str->String extraction (PyO3's own UTF-8 decoder raises builtins.UnicodeEncodeError) BEFORE to_rust runs, so the safety invariant (reject-before-writer-txn, no persistence) holds but the error CLASS is Python's UnicodeEncodeError, not the typed WriteValidationError that search's manual extract_validated_str yields. Making the pyo3 surrogate TYPED would require replacing PyProjectionSpec's auto-extracting #[new]/from_py_object String fields with Bound<PyAny> manual extraction — a pyclass extraction-model change beyond 'validate the strings that reach the spec'. Left as an explicit, flagged residual (NOT a leak): the named findings + the realistic NUL case are fully typed on both bindings; surrogate is safe on both. Recommend a follow-up ONLY if the reviewer wants surrogate error-class parity on the pyo3 input pyclass.
boundary_contract_audit.error_mapping: CONFORMANT — configure_projections / read_projections route through call_engine → engine_error_to_napi / the pyo3 mapper (no catch-all arm); ProjectionDestructiveError is typed + mapped (fix-3). No drift.
boundary_contract_audit.argument_validation: CONFORMANT — role strings are validated against the enum (from_str_opt → InvalidArgument) AND now FFI-validated. No id/int fields on the projection surface, so the negative-id checked_ids_napi / _validate_id_list guard is N/A. specs / drop lists have no count contract that other verbs impose.
boundary_contract_audit.before_writer_txn_ordering: CONFIRMED — in BOTH bindings the spec + drop validation runs synchronously before call_engine (which opens the writer transaction), satisfying AC-068's no-row-written invariant.
